oldMatchDict.docstring:
Reshape this Expr to a flat Series or a Series of Lists.

Parameters
----------
dims
    Tuple of the dimension sizes. If a -1 is used in any of the dimensions, that
    dimension is inferred.

Returns
-------
Expr
    If a single dimension is given, results in a flat Series of shape (len,).
    If a multiple dimensions are given, results in a Series of Lists with shape
    (rows, cols).

Examples
--------
>>> df = pl.DataFrame({"foo": [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9]})
>>> df.select(pl.col("foo").reshape((3, 3)))
shape: (3, 1)
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ foo       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ ---       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ list[i64] в”Ӯ
в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў
в”Ӯ [1, 2, 3] в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ [4, 5, 6] в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ [7, 8, 9] в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

See Also
--------
ExprListNameSpace.explode : Explode a list column.
oldMatchDict.sourceCode:
def reshape(self, dims: tuple[int, ...]) -> Self:
        """
        Reshape this Expr to a flat Series or a Series of Lists.

        Parameters
        ----------
        dims
            Tuple of the dimension sizes. If a -1 is used in any of the dimensions, that
            dimension is inferred.

        Returns
        -------
        Expr
            If a single dimension is given, results in a flat Series of shape (len,).
            If a multiple dimensions are given, results in a Series of Lists with shape
            (rows, cols).

        Examples
        --------
        >>> df = pl.DataFrame({"foo": [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9]})
        >>> df.select(pl.col("foo").reshape((3, 3)))
        shape: (3, 1)
        в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
        в”Ӯ foo       в”Ӯ
        в”Ӯ ---       в”Ӯ
        в”Ӯ list[i64] в”Ӯ
        в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў
        в”Ӯ [1, 2, 3] в”Ӯ
        в”Ӯ [4, 5, 6] в”Ӯ
        в”Ӯ [7, 8, 9] в”Ӯ
        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

        See Also
        --------
        ExprListNameSpace.explode : Explode a list column.

        """
        return self._from_pyexpr(self._pyexpr.reshape(dims))

newMatchDict.sourceCode:
def reshape(self, dimensions: tuple[int, ...]) -> Self:
        """
        Reshape this Expr to a flat Series or a Series of Lists.

        Parameters
        ----------
        dimensions
            Tuple of the dimension sizes. If a -1 is used in any of the dimensions, that
            dimension is inferred.

        Returns
        -------
        Expr
            If a single dimension is given, results in a flat Series of shape (len,).
            If a multiple dimensions are given, results in a Series of Lists with shape
            (rows, cols).

        Examples
        --------
        >>> df = pl.DataFrame({"foo": [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9]})
        >>> df.select(pl.col("foo").reshape((3, 3)))
        shape: (3, 1)
        в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
        в”Ӯ foo       в”Ӯ
        в”Ӯ ---       в”Ӯ
        в”Ӯ list[i64] в”Ӯ
        в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў
        в”Ӯ [1, 2, 3] в”Ӯ
        в”Ӯ [4, 5, 6] в”Ӯ
        в”Ӯ [7, 8, 9] в”Ӯ
        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

        See Also
        --------
        ExprListNameSpace.explode : Explode a list column.

        """
        return self._from_pyexpr(self._pyexpr.reshape(dimensions))
